title: Retry with Exponential Backoff
description:
Implement a robust data fetcher with configurable retry logic, exponential backoff, jitter, and cancellation support.

### Requirements

- Fetch data from an API endpoint. On failure, retry up to `maxRetries` times.
- Use exponential backoff: delay doubles each attempt (e.g., 1s → 2s → 4s)
- Add random jitter (±25%) to avoid thundering herd
- Show current attempt number during retries ("Attempt 2 of 3…")
- Show countdown timer for next retry ("Retrying in 3s…")
- Allow the user to cancel retries at any time
- When all retries are exhausted, show final error with a "Start Over" button
- Display request/response timeline log (each attempt with timestamp and result)

### Test IDs

| Element | data-testid |
|---------|-------------|
| Fetch button | `fetch-btn` |
| Cancel button | `cancel-btn` |
| Start over button | `start-over-btn` |
| Status text | `status-text` |
| Attempt info | `attempt-info` |
| Countdown | `countdown` |
| Data display | `data-display` |
| Timeline log | `timeline` |
| Timeline entry | `timeline-{index}` |
constraints:
- Backoff formula: `baseDelay * 2^(attempt-1)` with ±25% jitter
- Must support cancellation mid-retry
- Must track and display all attempts
- AbortController must be used for cancellation
- Jitter must be applied to prevent synchronized retries
hints: Use an AbortController to cancel both the fetch and the setTimeout between retries. Check `signal.aborted` before each attempt.
Store the timeline entries in state as `{ attempt, timestamp, status, message }[]`. Append after each attempt completes or fails.
